province.name: New Brunswick
notes: 2023 local-governance reform reorganized municipalities into 77 local governments + 12 rural districts; 2021 census geography (used here) predates it
Bay of Fundy has the highest tides on Earth
Saint John River (Wolastoq) runs the length of the western province
province.license_footer: Contains information licensed under the Open Government Licence – Canada.

Greater Moncton & Southeast.climate: Humid continental with a maritime edge: snowy freeze-thaw winters, warm summers, and Northumberland Strait shoreline that carries some of the warmest salt water north of Virginia. Strait-shore communities face coastal wind and documented storm-surge exposure; inland towns see heavy snow loads and spring melt.
Greater Moncton & Southeast.geology: Low-lying sandstone country of the Moncton sub-basin, tidal red-mud rivers off the Bay of Fundy (Petitcodiac, Memramcook), and salt-marsh/dune coast along the Northumberland Strait.
Greater Moncton & Southeast.economy: The fastest-growing urban region in Atlantic Canada: Greater Moncton is the Maritimes' transportation, logistics, retail and back-office hub, with Université de Moncton and two regional hospitals (The Moncton Hospital, Dr. Georges-L.-Dumont) anchoring a bilingual workforce. Kent County and the Strait shore run on fisheries, tourism and Acadian small business.
Greater Moncton & Southeast.buildingStock: Post-war bungalow neighbourhoods in Moncton and Riverview, fast-built 2010s–2020s vinyl-clad subdivisions in Dieppe and Moncton North, century farmhouses inland, and seasonal cottages thick along the Shediac–Cap-Pelé shore.
landmarks: Hopewell Rocks (Albert County, Bay of Fundy)

Fundy & Greater Saint John.climate: Bay of Fundy marine climate: cooler summers, frequent sea fog, salt air, and freeze-thaw winters. The Fundy coast is famously hard on building exteriors; the inland Kennebecasis Valley is warmer and drier in summer.
Fundy & Greater Saint John.geology: Rocky Fundy coastline with the world's highest tides, the drowned river valleys of the Saint John and Kennebecasis, and granite country around St. George in Charlotte County.
Fundy & Greater Saint John.economy: Saint John is the province's industrial anchor — the Irving Oil refinery (Canada's largest), Port Saint John container and cruise terminals, and the energy sector — while the Kennebecasis Valley towns are its commuter belt. Charlotte County runs on the Ganong chocolate legacy, aquaculture and fisheries, and resort tourism in St. Andrews.
Fundy & Greater Saint John.buildingStock: Pre-1900 uptown brick and wood heritage stock rebuilt after Saint John's Great Fire of 1877, post-war suburbs, 1970s–2000s Kennebecasis Valley subdivisions, and working fishing-village and island properties in Charlotte County.
landmarks: Saint John City Market (Canada's oldest continuing farmers' market)

Capital & River Valley.climate: Continental river-valley climate: cold snowy winters, warm summers, and a spring freshet on the Saint John River that produced major floods in 2018 and 2019 — flood-zone awareness is a real factor for riverfront property work.
Capital & River Valley.geology: The broad Saint John River (Wolastoq) valley: fertile floodplain and island farmland, forested Appalachian foothills, and the Mactaquac headpond above Fredericton.
Capital & River Valley.economy: Fredericton pairs provincial government with two universities (UNB and St. Thomas), an IT and cybersecurity sector, and RCMP J Division headquarters. Oromocto is CFB Gagetown, Canada's largest military training base. Carleton County upriver is the potato belt, headquartered by McCain Foods in Florenceville-Bristol.
Capital & River Valley.buildingStock: Government-town heritage streets and mid-century neighbourhoods in Fredericton, planned 1950s–60s housing in Oromocto, new commuter subdivisions in Hanwell and New Maryland, and farm properties and woodlot homes upriver.

Northwest & Madawaska.climate: The coldest, snowiest corner of New Brunswick: a true continental climate in the Appalachian uplands, with long winters, deep frost, and heavy snow loads on roofs — a genuine engineering factor for any exterior work.
Northwest & Madawaska.geology: Appalachian plateau cut by the upper Saint John River, with the Grand Falls gorge as its most dramatic feature and prime potato-growing soils on the benchlands.
Northwest & Madawaska.economy: Overwhelmingly francophone (the 'République du Madawaska' Brayon identity): Edmundston's Twin Rivers pulp and paper mill, a Université de Moncton campus, cross-border trade with Maine and Quebec, and the potato industry around Grand Falls and Saint-André.
Northwest & Madawaska.buildingStock: Early-20th-century mill-town housing in Edmundston, farmhouses and potato-farm outbuildings on the benchlands, and border-village homes strung along the upper Saint John River.
landmarks: Grand Falls gorge and its 23-metre cataract

Miramichi River Region.climate: Continental and snowy, with river ice and a hard spring runoff on the Miramichi system; summers are warm enough to make the river one of the world's storied Atlantic salmon fisheries.
Miramichi River Region.geology: The Miramichi River system — Main Southwest and Northwest branches — draining a vast forested interior to Miramichi Bay on the Gulf of St. Lawrence.
Miramichi River Region.economy: Built on forestry and the Atlantic salmon angling economy; the city of Miramichi has been rebuilding its base since the 2000s mill closures, with the river's outfitting, lodge and tourism trade a constant.
Miramichi River Region.buildingStock: Former mill-town neighbourhoods in the old Chatham and Newcastle town sites, riverfront camps and salmon lodges upriver, and Acadian coastal homes toward Neguac.

Acadian Peninsula & Chaleur.climate: Maritime climate on the Chaleur Bay and Gulf shore: winter sea ice, hard onshore winds, documented storm-surge exposure on low peninsula coasts, and short warm summers — conditions that punish exteriors faster than inland norms.
Acadian Peninsula & Chaleur.geology: Low peninsula country of beaches, salt marsh, barrier islands and the largest peat bogs in the province, fronting Chaleur Bay and the Gulf of St. Lawrence.
Acadian Peninsula & Chaleur.economy: The francophone heart of Acadie: snow crab and lobster fleets, peat harvesting around Shippagan and Lamèque, fish processing, and a strong cultural-tourism economy centred on Caraquet. Bathurst, the regional service city, has been diversifying since the Brunswick zinc mine closed in 2013.
Acadian Peninsula & Chaleur.buildingStock: Fishing-village homes and wharf-side buildings, seasonal shore cottages, and post-war housing in Bathurst's service-city neighbourhoods.
landmarks: Festival acadien de Caraquet and the August 15 Tintamarre
New Brunswick Aquarium and Marine Centre (Shippagan)

Restigouche & Chaleur Bay.climate: Appalachian snowbelt: the heaviest, longest winters in the province alongside the Northwest, with deep snowpack (Sugarloaf's ski hill runs on it) and a hard freshet on the Restigouche system.
Restigouche & Chaleur Bay.geology: Mountainous Appalachian terrain dropping to the Restigouche River estuary and Chaleur Bay — the most rugged relief in New Brunswick.
Restigouche & Chaleur Bay.economy: Forestry country and world-famous Restigouche salmon water. Campbellton is the border-city gateway to Quebec's Gaspé Peninsula; Dalhousie has been reinventing itself since its paper mill closed in 2008; Saint-Quentin claims the title of maple capital of Atlantic Canada.
Restigouche & Chaleur Bay.buildingStock: Early-20th-century mill-town housing in Campbellton and Dalhousie, rural forestry properties, and camp and lodge stock along the Restigouche.
landmarks: J.C. Van Horne Bridge to Quebec (Campbellton)

moncton.hook: Canada's first officially bilingual city and the fastest-growing urban centre in Atlantic Canada — the Maritimes' transportation and logistics hub on the tidal Petitcodiac.
facts: Canada's first officially bilingual city
Petitcodiac River tidal bore runs through downtown
Magnetic Hill optical-illusion landmark and Magic Mountain water park
Avenir Centre arena anchors the downtown
Université de Moncton is the largest French-language university outside Quebec
Major employers include Medavie Blue Cross, the two regional hospitals and the CN rail yard
Resurgo Place museum tells the city's railway story
moncton.geoTrait: tidal Petitcodiac River bend and the Moncton sub-basin lowlands

saint-john.hook: Canada's first incorporated city (1785) and New Brunswick's industrial anchor — refinery, port and fog-wrapped uptown brick on the Bay of Fundy.
facts: Canada's first incorporated city, 1785
Irving Oil refinery is Canada's largest
Port Saint John handles container and cruise traffic
Reversing Falls Rapids, where Fundy tides push the Saint John River backwards
Uptown's brick streetscape was rebuilt after the Great Fire of 1877
Saint John City Market is Canada's oldest continuing farmers' market
Rockwood Park is one of Canada's largest urban parks
Imperial Theatre and the New Brunswick Museum anchor the cultural core
saint-john.geoTrait: Bay of Fundy harbour at the mouth of the Saint John River, wrapped in marine fog

dieppe.hook: New Brunswick's fastest-growing major city and its largest francophone-majority municipality — home to the Greater Moncton airport and the region's retail engine.
facts: Greater Moncton Roméo LeBlanc International Airport is in Dieppe
CF Champlain (Champlain Place) is the region's largest shopping centre
Dieppe Farmers' Market
Fox Creek Golf Club
Named in honour of the 1942 Dieppe Raid
dieppe.geoTrait: flat sandstone lowlands between the Petitcodiac River and the Northumberland Strait road corridor

riverview.hook: The bedroom town across the Petitcodiac from Moncton — tidal riverfront marshes, post-war family streets and the Gunningsville Bridge commute.
facts: Faces Moncton across the tidal Petitcodiac River
Gunningsville Bridge (opened 2005) ties downtown Riverview to downtown Moncton
Mill Creek Nature Park
Riverfront marsh trails along the Petitcodiac
Gateway toward Fundy Albert County and Hopewell Rocks
riverview.geoTrait: south bank of the tidal Petitcodiac River, backed by Albert County marsh and woodland

fredericton.hook: New Brunswick's capital: government town, two universities, a riverfront Garrison District — and hard-earned flood-zone expertise on the Saint John River.
facts: Provincial capital
University of New Brunswick (founded 1785, among Canada's oldest universities) and St. Thomas University
Historic Garrison District and Officers' Square
Beaverbrook Art Gallery
RCMP J Division headquarters
Growing IT and cybersecurity sector
Odell Park preserves old-growth forest inside the city
Bill Thorpe Walking Bridge spans the Saint John River
Major Saint John River floods hit riverfront neighbourhoods in 2018 and 2019
North-side neighbourhoods include the historic cotton-mill town of Marysville, a National Historic Site
fredericton.geoTrait: Saint John River (Wolastoq) valley flats with flood-aware riverfront neighbourhoods

quispamsis.hook: The Kennebecasis Valley's biggest commuter town — one of the province's fastest-growing suburban communities, strung along river coves northeast of Saint John.
facts: Largest of Saint John's Kennebecasis Valley commuter towns
qplex recreation complex
Meenan's Cove beach and park on the Kennebecasis River
Absorbed Gondola Point in the 1998 amalgamation
quispamsis.geoTrait: coves and low hills on the Kennebecasis River northeast of Saint John

rothesay.hook: The Kennebecasis Valley's establishment address — riverfront estates, the Rothesay Netherwood School campus and a common on the river.
facts: Rothesay Netherwood School, a historic independent boarding school
Rothesay Common and riverfront wharf area at Renforth
Formed in 1998 by amalgamating Rothesay, Renforth, East Riverside-Kingshurst and Fairvale
Among the province's highest-income communities
rothesay.geoTrait: west-facing Kennebecasis riverfront with mature estate lots

miramichi.hook: The city the salmon river built — formed in 1995 from the rival towns of Chatham and Newcastle, and still the world's most famous Atlantic salmon address.
facts: Created in 1995 by amalgamating Chatham, Newcastle, Douglastown, Loggieville and Nelson
World-renowned Atlantic salmon angling on the Miramichi River
Beaubears Island national historic sites sit midstream
Ritchie Wharf park and French Fort Cove
Lord Beaverbrook (Max Aitken) was raised in Newcastle and is commemorated in its town square
Rebuilding its economy since the 2000s pulp-mill closures
miramichi.geoTrait: twin historic town sites facing each other across the broad Miramichi River

tracadie.hook: The Acadian Peninsula's regional municipality — New Brunswick's first, created in 2014 — with a working fishing coast and a singular medical history.
facts: Became New Brunswick's first regional municipality in 2014
Historic lazaretto: Tracadie housed Canada's only leprosarium, remembered at the Musée historique de Tracadie
Fishing and peat-country service centre for the eastern Peninsula
Includes the community of Sheila
tracadie.geoTrait: low Gulf-shore coast at the Tracadie River mouth, fringed with beaches and peat bogs

shediac.hook: The self-proclaimed Lobster Capital of the World — giant lobster statue included — where Parlee Beach makes summer the town's biggest industry.
facts: Lobster Capital of the World, marked by the giant lobster sculpture
Parlee Beach Provincial Park draws big summer crowds to the warm Northumberland Strait
Pointe-du-Chêne wharf
Population swells dramatically each summer with cottagers and tourists
shediac.geoTrait: warm-water Northumberland Strait beach coast at Shediac Bay

beaubassin-east.hook: A string of Acadian shore communities between Shediac and Cap-Pelé — cottage coast, salt marsh and one of the region's fastest-growing rural populations.
facts: Rural community stretching along the Northumberland Strait shore between Shediac and Cap-Pelé
Includes the communities of Grand-Barachois and Haute-Aboujagane
Strait-shore cottage country with year-round conversion pressure
beaubassin-east.geoTrait: barrier-beach and salt-marsh shore of the Northumberland Strait

bathurst.hook: The Chaleur Bay service city rebuilding after a century of mining — home to the K.C. Irving Regional Centre and the harbour at the Nepisiguit's mouth.
facts: Regional service centre for northern New Brunswick
Brunswick Mine, once among the world's largest zinc mines, closed in 2013
K.C. Irving Regional Centre hosts the QMJHL's Acadie-Bathurst Titan
Youghall Beach on Bathurst Harbour
Daly Point Nature Reserve salt marsh
CCNB college campus
bathurst.geoTrait: sheltered harbour where the Nepisiguit River meets Chaleur Bay

sackville.hook: A university town on the Tantramar marshes — Mount Allison's campus, a famous waterfowl park, and the last stop before Nova Scotia.
facts: Home of Mount Allison University
Sackville Waterfowl Park's boardwalks wind through the town
Sits on the Tantramar Marshes near the Nova Scotia border
Historic town core of brick and clapboard around the university
sackville.geoTrait: edge of the vast Tantramar marshland at the head of the Bay of Fundy

oromocto.hook: Canada's Model Town — planned and built in the late 1950s to serve CFB Gagetown, the country's largest military training base.
facts: Purpose-built planned town of the late 1950s, nicknamed 'Canada's Model Town'
CFB Gagetown is Canada's largest military training base
Sits where the Oromocto River meets the Saint John
Large rotating military population shapes the housing market
oromocto.geoTrait: confluence of the Oromocto and Saint John rivers beside CFB Gagetown

memramcook.hook: The cradle of the Acadian renaissance — the Monument-Lefebvre and the old Collège Saint-Joseph stand over a farming valley that never emptied.
facts: Monument-Lefebvre National Historic Site
Site of Collège Saint-Joseph, the first French-language degree-granting college in Atlantic Canada
Fertile Memramcook Valley farmland
One of the oldest continuously settled Acadian districts
memramcook.geoTrait: sheltered agricultural valley of the tidal Memramcook River

edmundston.hook: Capital of the 'République du Madawaska' — a proudly Brayon mill city wedged between Quebec and Maine, wrapped around the Twin Rivers pulp mill.
facts: Twin Rivers Paper pulp mill anchors the economy
Université de Moncton's Edmundston campus
Overwhelmingly francophone, with a distinct Brayon identity
Foire Brayonne festival each summer
New Brunswick Botanical Garden
Petit-Témis cycling trail runs to Rivière-du-Loup, Quebec
Amalgamated Saint-Basile and Saint-Jacques in 1998
Borders Madawaska, Maine, across the Saint John River
edmundston.geoTrait: Appalachian valley at the junction of the Madawaska and Saint John rivers, on the Quebec–Maine border

grand-bay-westfield.hook: River-mouth commuter town where the Saint John widens toward Grand Bay — cable ferries, river coves and a 1998 twin-community amalgamation.
facts: Formed in 1998 by amalgamating Grand Bay and Westfield
Free river cable ferries connect the Kingston Peninsula nearby
Commuter town on the Saint John River's Grand Bay reach
grand-bay-westfield.geoTrait: wooded shoreline where the Saint John River opens into Grand Bay

campbellton.hook: The border city under Sugarloaf — Restigouche salmon water out front, a ski hill out back, and the J.C. Van Horne Bridge to Quebec downtown.
facts: Restigouche Sam, the giant Atlantic salmon statue
Sugarloaf Provincial Park and its ski hill rise over the city
J.C. Van Horne Bridge crosses to Pointe-à-la-Croix, Quebec
Gateway to Quebec's Gaspé Peninsula
Restigouche River is world-famous salmon water
campbellton.geoTrait: foot of Sugarloaf Mountain on the Restigouche River estuary

hampton.hook: Shiretown of Kings County on the Kennebecasis marshes — and hometown of John Peters Humphrey, who drafted the Universal Declaration of Human Rights.
facts: Shiretown of Kings County
Birthplace of John Peters Humphrey, principal drafter of the UN Universal Declaration of Human Rights, honoured with a monument
Kennebecasis River marshes frame the town
Commuter town at the east end of the Kennebecasis Valley
hampton.geoTrait: low marshland bend of the Kennebecasis River

woodstock.hook: New Brunswick's first incorporated town (1856) — the upper-valley crossroads where the Trans-Canada meets the road to Maine.
facts: New Brunswick's first incorporated town, 1856
Shiretown of Carleton County with a historic court house
Junction of the Trans-Canada Highway and Route 95 to Houlton, Maine
Service centre for the upper Saint John River potato belt
woodstock.geoTrait: confluence of the Meduxnekeag and Saint John rivers in the upper valley

cocagne.hook: An Acadian harbour community on the Cocagne River estuary — marina country between Shediac and Bouctouche.
facts: Rural community on the Cocagne River estuary and Northumberland Strait
Marina and boating harbour
Part of the Acadian coastal corridor between Shediac and Bouctouche
cocagne.geoTrait: sheltered estuary where the Cocagne River meets the Strait

sussex.hook: The dairy town that became Atlantic Canada's mural capital — covered-bridge country in the Kennebecasis farm valley, adjusting since the 2016 potash shutdown.
facts: Historic dairy-farming centre of the Kennebecasis valley
Dozens of outdoor murals earned it the mural-capital title
Atlantic Balloon Fiesta hosts hot-air balloons each fall
Nearby potash mine suspended operations in 2016, reshaping the local economy
Covered bridges dot the surrounding valley
sussex.geoTrait: broad dairy-farm valley of the upper Kennebecasis River

bouctouche.hook: K.C. Irving's hometown and Antonine Maillet's Acadie — the Bouctouche dune, La Sagouine's village, and a harbour town that punches far above its size.
facts: Birthplace of industrialist K.C. Irving
Le Pays de la Sagouine theatrical village celebrates author Antonine Maillet's work
Irving Eco-Centre protects the 12-km Bouctouche dune
Acadian harbour town on Bouctouche Bay
bouctouche.geoTrait: harbour behind the great sand dune of Bouctouche Bay

hanwell.hook: Fredericton's newest commuter corridor — a rural community incorporated in 2014 that has been growing as fast as anywhere in the capital region.
facts: Incorporated as a rural community in 2014
Strung along the Hanwell Road (Route 640) corridor southwest of Fredericton
One of the capital region's fastest-growing communities
hanwell.geoTrait: wooded upland corridor along Route 640 southwest of Fredericton

st-stephen.hook: Canada's Chocolate Town — Ganong Bros., the country's oldest candy maker, still runs here, a footbridge from Calais, Maine.
facts: Home of Ganong Bros., Canada's oldest candy company
The Chocolate Museum and annual Chocolate Fest
International border town facing Calais, Maine, across the St. Croix River
Key Canada–US border crossing in Charlotte County
st-stephen.geoTrait: St. Croix River border crossing to Maine

grand-falls.hook: The town built around a 23-metre cataract — the Saint John River plunges through a mile-long gorge right beside the bilingual main street.
facts: The Saint John River drops 23 metres at the falls and runs a 1.6-km gorge through town
Officially bilingual name: Grand Falls / Grand-Sault
Centre of the upper-valley potato industry
Gorge walking trails and viewing platforms
grand-falls.geoTrait: gorge and cataract of the Saint John River

salisbury.hook: The village at the western gate of Greater Moncton — Petitcodiac headwater country with a silver-fox-farming heritage.
facts: Sits at the junction of Route 112 and the Trans-Canada west of Moncton
Historic centre of early-20th-century silver-fox fur farming
Commuter village on the Petitcodiac River's upper reaches

saint-andrews.hook: St. Andrews-by-the-Sea: Canada's original seaside resort town — the Algonquin's turrets, Kingsbrae Garden and whale boats off the wharf.
facts: One of Canada's oldest seaside resort towns, a National Historic District
The Algonquin Resort has hosted guests since 1889
Kingsbrae Garden
Whale-watching and Huntsman Marine Science Centre
Loyalist-era street grid on Passamaquoddy Bay
saint-andrews.geoTrait: Passamaquoddy Bay peninsula with a Loyalist-era grid

st-george.hook: The Granite Town on the Magaguadavic — a gorge and falls in the middle of the village and Lake Utopia (monster folklore included) out back.
facts: Known as the Granite Town for its red-granite quarrying history
Magaguadavic River falls and gorge cut through the village
Lake Utopia nearby, famous for its lake-monster folklore
Service centre for Charlotte County's Fundy shore
st-george.geoTrait: granite gorge of the Magaguadavic River

richibucto.hook: The shiretown of Kent County — a fishing port at the Richibucto River mouth, minutes from Kouchibouguac National Park.
facts: Historic shiretown of Kent County
Fishing port on the Richibucto River estuary
Gateway to Kouchibouguac National Park
richibucto.geoTrait: estuary port at the mouth of the Richibucto River

cap-pele.hook: The village that smokes the world's herring — dozens of family 'boucanières' (smokehouses) and Aboiteau Beach on the warm Strait.
facts: Famous for its herring smokehouses ('boucanières'), producing much of the world's smoked herring
Aboiteau Beach on the Northumberland Strait
Strongly Acadian fishing and cottage community
cap-pele.geoTrait: smokehouse-lined shore of the Northumberland Strait

new-maryland.hook: The capital's quiet southern bedroom village — leafy subdivisions on the height of land just past Fredericton's city line.
facts: Bedroom village immediately south of Fredericton
Grew up along the Route 101 corridor
Predominantly single-family subdivision housing
new-maryland.geoTrait: upland plateau just south of Fredericton

saint-antoine.hook: Birthplace of Louis J. Robichaud — the premier who transformed New Brunswick — and a growing Acadian village inland from the Strait coast.
facts: Birthplace of Louis J. Robichaud, New Brunswick's first elected Acadian premier
Inland Kent County village within commuting range of Moncton
Strongly francophone community
saint-antoine.geoTrait: inland Kent County farm and forest country

hillsborough.hook: A gypsum-country village on the Petitcodiac with a working railway museum — and Hopewell Rocks twenty minutes down the road.
facts: New Brunswick Railway Museum and the Salem & Hillsborough heritage railroad
Long history of gypsum quarrying
Birthplace of William Steeves, a Father of Confederation
Twenty minutes from Hopewell Rocks on the Fundy shore
hillsborough.geoTrait: gypsum hills over the tidal Petitcodiac

petitcodiac.hook: A railway-junction village turned commuter crossroads at the head of the Petitcodiac valley, between Moncton and Sussex.
facts: Grew up as a railway junction village
Sits at the head of the Petitcodiac River valley on Route 106
Farm-service village within commuting range of both Moncton and Sussex
petitcodiac.geoTrait: headwater valley of the Petitcodiac River

upper-miramichi.hook: Boiestown country — the rural community at the geographic centre of New Brunswick, where the Woodsmen's Museum keeps the lumber-camp story alive.
facts: Centred on Boiestown, commonly cited as the geographic centre of New Brunswick
Central New Brunswick Woodsmen's Museum
Headwater salmon-angling and forestry country on the Main Southwest Miramichi
Incorporated as a rural community in 2008
upper-miramichi.geoTrait: forested headwaters of the Main Southwest Miramichi River

grand-manan.hook: The Bay of Fundy's big island — dulse from Dark Harbour, the Swallowtail light, and a 90-minute ferry that sets the rhythm of everything.
facts: Reached by a 90-minute ferry from Blacks Harbour
Dark Harbour is famous for hand-harvested dulse
Swallowtail Lighthouse
Lobster and scallop fishing anchor the economy
Island logistics shape every trade and delivery
grand-manan.geoTrait: cliff-edged Fundy island with working harbours

caraquet.hook: The cultural capital of Acadie — the Festival acadien since 1963, the Tintamarre every August 15, and a working fishing port on Chaleur Bay.
facts: Widely called the capital of Acadie
Festival acadien de Caraquet, running since 1963, climaxes in the August 15 Tintamarre
Village Historique Acadien living-history site is just west of town
Active fishing port on Chaleur Bay

norton.hook: A Kennebecasis valley farm village midway between Sussex and Hampton — quiet, agricultural, and increasingly a commuter address.
facts: Farm village on the Kennebecasis River between Sussex and Hampton
Within commuting range of both Saint John and Sussex
norton.geoTrait: mid-valley farmland on the Kennebecasis River

beresford.hook: Bathurst's beach-town neighbour on Chaleur Bay — a saltmarsh-and-sand shoreline with one of the north shore's best beaches.
facts: Beresford Beach and its protected saltmarsh
Effectively a residential and cottage extension of the Bathurst area
Francophone Chaleur-shore community
beresford.geoTrait: sand and saltmarsh shore of Chaleur Bay

minto.hook: One of North America's oldest coal-mining districts — a Grand Lake village remaking itself after the mines and the power station closed.
facts: Coal was mined in the Minto–Grand Lake field for centuries — among the earliest coal workings in North America
Sits near Grand Lake, the province's largest lake
Post-mining economy now leans on forestry and commuting

sussex-corner.hook: The village at Sussex's shoulder — where the dairy valley climbs toward the Fundy hills and covered-bridge back roads.
facts: Contiguous with the town of Sussex
Covered bridges survive on nearby valley roads
Dairy-farm and commuter village in the upper Kennebecasis valley
sussex-corner.geoTrait: valley shoulder where the Kennebecasis meets the Fundy hills

shippagan.hook: Canada's peat capital and the Acadian Peninsula's campus town — a snow-crab port with the province's marine aquarium.
facts: Centre of Canada's peat-moss industry
New Brunswick Aquarium and Marine Centre
Université de Moncton's Shippagan campus
Major snow-crab fishing port
shippagan.geoTrait: low peat-bog peninsula tip facing Lamèque Island

chipman.hook: A Salmon River forestry village at the top of Grand Lake — woods work, water access and a long lumbering story.
facts: Forestry village on the Salmon River near Grand Lake's northern end
Long history in lumbering and wood processing
chipman.geoTrait: Salmon River lowlands at the head of Grand Lake

rogersville.hook: An Acadian village with a Trappist monastery at its edge — quiet farm country halfway between Moncton and Miramichi.
facts: Notre-Dame du Calvaire Trappist monastery
Acadian farming village on the Moncton–Miramichi corridor (Route 126)
Annual blueberry festival celebrates the local crop
rogersville.geoTrait: blueberry and farm flats on the inland Kent–Northumberland line

florenceville-bristol.hook: The French-fry capital of the world — McCain Foods' global headquarters in a two-village town over the Saint John River potato belt.
facts: World headquarters of McCain Foods, the world's largest french-fry producer
Formed in 2008 by merging Florenceville and Bristol
Heart of the Carleton County potato belt
florenceville-bristol.geoTrait: potato-belt benchlands over the Saint John River

campobello-island.hook: FDR's beloved island — Roosevelt Campobello International Park at one end, Head Harbour Light at the other, and year-round road access only through Maine.
facts: Roosevelt Campobello International Park preserves FDR's summer home
Head Harbour Lightstation
The FDR Memorial Bridge to Lubec, Maine, is the only year-round road access
Fishing community in the Passamaquoddy islands
campobello-island.geoTrait: outer Passamaquoddy island reached by bridge from Maine

mcadam.hook: The railway village a station built — McAdam's chateau-style CPR station is a National Historic Site, and the town still gathers around it.
facts: McAdam Railway Station, a chateau-style CPR landmark, is a National Historic Site
Grew as a Canadian Pacific Railway junction near the Maine border
Forest-country village in southwestern York County
mcadam.geoTrait: rail-junction clearing in the southwestern York County woods

neguac.hook: An Acadian harbour village where Miramichi Bay meets the open Gulf — wharf, beach and a strong fishing tradition.
facts: Acadian fishing village on Miramichi Bay
Working wharf and beach on the inner bay
Northern gateway between the Miramichi and the Acadian Peninsula
neguac.geoTrait: sheltered inner shore of Miramichi Bay

dorchester.hook: The old shiretown of Westmorland County — sandstone heritage buildings, a federal penitentiary, and world-class shorebird flats at Johnson's Mills.
facts: Historic shiretown of Westmorland County
Dorchester Penitentiary, one of Canada's oldest federal prisons
Keillor House Museum and sandstone heritage architecture
Johnson's Mills shorebird reserve on the Fundy mudflats nearby
dorchester.geoTrait: sandstone ridge over the Memramcook estuary marshes

dalhousie.hook: A deep-water port town reinventing itself since the 2008 mill closure — Inch Arran point, ferry views to Quebec and Restigouche estuary light.
facts: Deep-water port on the Restigouche estuary
Paper mill closed in 2008, reshaping the town's economy
Inch Arran lighthouse and shorefront
Seasonal ferry link to Miguasha, Quebec
dalhousie.geoTrait: point of land where the Restigouche estuary opens into Chaleur Bay

lameque.hook: The island town of the peat bogs — and, improbably and wonderfully, host of an internationally known Baroque music festival.
facts: Island community connected by causeway/bridge via Shippagan
Surrounded by commercial peat bogs
Hosts the International Baroque Music Festival each summer
lameque.geoTrait: flat peat-bog island between Chaleur Bay and the Gulf

saint-quentin.hook: The maple capital of Atlantic Canada — a forestry town in the high Restigouche with a famous western festival.
facts: Claims the title of maple capital of Atlantic Canada
Festival Western de Saint-Quentin draws crowds each July
Forestry and maple-sugar economy in the Appalachian uplands
saint-quentin.geoTrait: high Appalachian plateau of maple woods

nackawic.hook: The mill town with the World's Largest Axe — built in the 1960s when the Mactaquac headpond rose, and still running on its pulp mill.
facts: World's Largest Axe monument on the waterfront
Town created in the 1960s alongside the Mactaquac headpond
AV Group NBI pulp mill is the anchor employer
nackawic.geoTrait: terraced townsite over the Mactaquac headpond of the Saint John River

blacks-harbour.hook: The sardine village — long home to Connors Bros. and the mainland terminal for the Grand Manan ferry.
facts: Home of Connors Bros., historically one of the world's largest sardine processors
Mainland terminal for the Grand Manan ferry
Working Fundy fishing harbour
blacks-harbour.geoTrait: working harbour cut into the Fundy shore

rexton.hook: Birthplace of Bonar Law — the only British prime minister born outside the British Isles — on the tidal Richibucto.
facts: Birthplace of Andrew Bonar Law, UK prime minister 1922–23
Village on the tidal Richibucto River across from Richibucto
Bonar Law historic site commemorates the family homestead
rexton.geoTrait: tidal river flats of the lower Richibucto

haut-madawaska.hook: Five border villages made one — Clair, Baker-Brook and their neighbours along the upper Saint John, facing Maine across the river.
facts: Formed in 2017 by amalgamating border villages including Clair, Baker-Brook, Saint-Hilaire and Saint-François
Faces Maine across the upper Saint John River
Farm and forest communities of the Madawaska uplands
haut-madawaska.geoTrait: upper Saint John River border bench facing Maine

saint-andre.hook: Potato country north of Grand Falls — a young rural community of big farms on the upper-valley benchlands.
facts: Potato-farming rural community just north of Grand Falls
Part of the upper Saint John River agricultural belt
saint-andre.geoTrait: open potato benchlands above the upper Saint John River

gagetown.hook: The riverside shiretown of Queens County — artisan village, cable ferry, and not to be confused with the base that borrowed its name.
facts: Historic shiretown of Queens County on the Saint John River
Gagetown–Jemseg area cable ferry crossing
Known for artisan studios and a heritage riverfront
Distinct from CFB Gagetown, which is located at Oromocto
gagetown.geoTrait: riverbank shiretown reach of the lower Saint John

saint-louis-de-kent.hook: Where the Acadian flag comes from — Father Marcel-François Richard's village at the gates of Kouchibouguac National Park.
facts: Home village of Father Marcel-François Richard, who designed the Acadian tricolour flag
Gateway community for Kouchibouguac National Park
Acadian village on Route 134 in northern Kent County
saint-louis-de-kent.geoTrait: wooded coastal plain at the edge of Kouchibouguac National Park

cambridge-narrows.hook: Washademoak Lake cottage country — a narrows-spanning village where lake life is the local economy.
facts: Village spanning the narrows of Washademoak Lake
Long-established cottage and boating community
Seasonal properties make up a large share of the housing stock

kedgwick.hook: Deep-woods forestry country on the storied Restigouche — canoe water, hunting camps and working timberland.
facts: Forestry community in the Restigouche uplands
Access point to the Restigouche River's famous canoe and salmon waters
kedgwick.geoTrait: high timberland plateau above the Restigouche River

fredericton-junction.hook: The village the railways crossed — a historic junction on the Saint John–Fredericton line, now quiet commuter country on the South Branch Oromocto.
facts: Grew up as a railway junction on the Saint John–Fredericton line
Sits on the South Branch Oromocto River
Within commuting range of both Fredericton and Oromocto
fredericton-junction.geoTrait: rail-junction village on the South Branch Oromocto River

petit-rocher.hook: An Acadian shore village on the Chaleur coast with a wharf at its heart and mining history in its hills.
facts: Acadian fishing village between Bathurst and Belledune on the Chaleur shore
Working wharf and beach
Area's zinc-mining history is interpreted locally
petit-rocher.geoTrait: wharf-centred shore terrace on Chaleur Bay

hartland.hook: Home of the world's longest covered bridge — 391 metres across the Saint John — and the potato-chip factory that borrowed its fame.
facts: World's longest covered bridge, 391 m, crosses the Saint John River here
Covered Bridge Potato Chips is made in Hartland
Small service town in the Carleton County potato belt
hartland.geoTrait: covered-bridge crossing of the Saint John River

tracy.hook: A South Branch Oromocto village in the woods south of Fredericton — small, quiet and squarely in the capital's commuter orbit.
facts: Village on the South Branch Oromocto River adjacent to Fredericton Junction
Rural commuter village south of Fredericton
tracy.geoTrait: forest-and-river village on the South Branch Oromocto
